Tous Les Jours · Luxury Fragrance Campaign 2026

CROWN

Wear Your Crown Every Day

Creative Strategy & Brand Experience Team

01
CROWN
Brand Opportunity

Every person deserves
to feel royal.

Today's luxury consumer no longer buys fragrance alone. They buy a feeling that follows them through the day — and CROWN can become the vessel for it.

They are purchasing:

  • Confidence
  • Identity
  • Status
  • Emotion
  • Self-expression

Our mission: elevate brand perception while keeping luxury within reach.

02
CROWN
Product DNA

Luxury Eau
de Parfum

Size

100ml · 3.4 fl oz

Family

Oriental Gourmand

Top citrus accord · Heart white flowers · Base caramel, amber, musk, vanilla

Affordable luxury for everyday life

03
CROWN
Design Language Analysis

Luxury is read before it is smelled.

Burgundy

Sophistication · Passion · Exclusivity

Gold

Prestige · Luxury · Confidence

Crystal Glass

Purity · Transparency · Refinement

Deep burgundy, a geometric crystal facet pattern, a metallic gold label and jewel-inspired cap. The pack already signals premium — the campaign amplifies that perception across every touchpoint.

04
CROWN
Who We Speak To

25–45 · Unisex

"I want luxury without paying luxury prices."

Professionals · Entrepreneurs · Luxury seekers · Fashion enthusiasts · Premium lifestyle consumers.

Region · GCC

United Arab Emirates Saudi Arabia Qatar Kuwait

A region that prizes fragrance as identity — where everyday ritual meets aspiration.

05
The Big Idea

WEAR YOUR
CROWN
EVERY DAY

Luxury is not an occasion. It's a signature.

Everyday moments become royal moments. The fragrance becomes the invisible crown people wear daily.

06
CROWN
Creative Strategy

The name is the territory.

CROWN gives us an emotional symbol that needs no explanation. The campaign turns the product into a marker of:

Confidence

Elegance

Personal success

Everyday luxury

The consumer becomes the king or queen of their own story.

07
CROWN
The World of Crown

Modern royalty,
golden hour.

A royal-inspired penthouse above the Abu Dhabi skyline. Warm, cinematic, editorial. Reference codes drawn from Dior, Tom Ford, Chanel and YSL Beauty.

Mood
Warm
Light
Golden Hour
Tone
Editorial

Hero Visual Concept

Product is the hero.
Human builds aspiration.

  • Royal architecture · editorial composition
